section.title: AI in global health
section.intro: AI tools built for or deployed in global health systems — disease surveillance, diagnostics, outbreak response, and field epidemiology.

resources.title: BEACON: Open-Source AI Surveillance
resources.description: Open-source surveillance system using PandemIQ Llama (custom LLM) to scan global data streams for infectious disease signals.

resources.title: WHO EIOS 2.0
resources.description: AI-powered epidemic intelligence. Processes news, social media, and radio (speech-to-text) across 110+ countries for early outbreak detection.

resources.title: DHIS2 AI Integrations
resources.description: AI alert triage in Tanzania: disease alert processing from 36 hours to near-instant. Also: NLP queries for non-technical users and CHAP climate-health forecasting.

resources.title: SORMAS + SORMAS.AI
resources.description: Open-source outbreak management system deployed across African health systems. SORMAS.AI adds predictive modeling for case classification and outbreak trajectory.

resources.title: qXR (Qure.ai) — AI TB Screening
resources.description: WHO-recommended AI tool for TB X-ray screening. Deployed in 40+ countries alongside CAD4TB and Lunit INSIGHT CXR.

resources.title: iMAGING — Solar-Powered Malaria Microscopy
resources.description: Automated malaria diagnosis in 3 minutes 48 seconds. Solar-powered, works offline. Designed for settings where trained microscopists aren't available.

resources.title: AMRFinderPlus
resources.description: NCBI's genomic AMR detection tool. Identifies resistance genes and point mutations from assembled genomes or nucleotide sequences.

resources.title: SaTScan — Cluster Detection
resources.description: Spatial, temporal, and space-time cluster detection for disease surveillance. Free. Standard FETP tool for outbreak investigation.

resources.title: Delve — AI Qualitative Analysis
resources.description: Qualitative analysis tool with AI peer debriefer feature. Designed for thematic analysis workflows.

resources.title: ReDPeTT — FETP Project Tracking
resources.description: FETP project tracking system becoming mandatory in 2026. Replaces ad hoc tracking across field epidemiology training programs.
